items.front: What three elements must be included in landing information?
What must nonapproach control towers forward to arriving aircraft?
items.back: Approach information such as the type of approach to expect.

items.front: When can the next arrival cross the landing threshold?
items.back: Only after the preceding aircraft has cleared the runway.

items.front: What is the primary separation concern for intersecting runway arrivals?
items.back: Aircraft must not arrive at the runway intersection at the same time.

items.front: When may a landing clearance be issued even if the pilot cannot see the runway?
items.back: When the pilot can see the approach lights or other identifiable portions of the airport.

items.front: What does anticipating separation allow for arrivals?
items.back: Issuing a landing clearance when the preceding aircraft will clear the runway before the arrival crosses the threshold.

items.front: When can a landing clearance be issued without visual observation of the runway?
items.back: When separation is met and the pilot reports the runway in sight or the controller can otherwise determine it is clear.

items.front: When must a landing clearance be withheld?
items.back: When the runway is occupied by another aircraft or vehicle, or other safety conditions prevent safe landing.

items.front: How should exiting the runway be handled after landing?
items.back: Issue specific instructions for exiting, including which taxiway to use.

items.front: What is an altitude restricted low approach?
items.back: A low approach with a specified altitude restriction lower than the published minimum altitude.

items.front: What must be established before clearing aircraft in closed traffic?
items.back: The sequence of arriving and departing aircraft.

items.front: How does an overhead maneuver approach work?
items.back: Aircraft approaches from upwind, then breaks to downwind for landing. It's a military pattern maneuver.
Simulated Flameout — a practice maneuver simulating engine failure with a steep descent to landing. Not an actual emergency.
